Prohibitive framing likely to override the positive cli-dispatch instruction, causing either refusal or silent substitution.
remediation_sketch: (a) Fix cli-dispatch Section 3 to use {AGENTS_DIR}/{agent-id}.md (see LEGION-47-121 target), OR (b) relax this rule to 'Use {AGENTS_DIR}/{agent-id}.md as canonical; bare accepted in local-dev'. Path (a) is correct.

excerpt: DIVISIONS = [ ... Engineering 7 agents ... Marketing 8 agents ... Testing 7 agents ... Support 6 agents ... ] TOTAL_AGENTS = 52
issue: Three conflicting agent counts: sum = 51, TOTAL_AGENTS declared = 52, CLAUDE.md Dynamic Knowledge Index = 48. No rule for which is canonical. Downstream agents injected with TOTAL_AGENTS=52 will assume a larger roster than reality, leading to invented agent IDs (same defect class as LEGION-47-052 non-existent agent ID).
remediation_sketch: Replace hardcoded counts with filesystem lookup: 'Run: ls {AGENTS_DIR}/*.md | wc -l for authoritative count; divisions read from agent frontmatter.' Add /legion:validate check comparing declared counts to filesystem reality.

excerpt: When commands or skills reference adapter.ask_user ... you MUST use the AskUserQuestion tool ... This applies to ALL question prompts in ALL commands
issue: Inheriting S03 cross-cut. Canonical declaration of user-interaction contract in always-load core. 'ALL question prompts in ALL commands' is absolute; AskUserQuestion requires non-empty options. But many commands capture free-text (project vision, task prompts) with no bounded options. Readers forced to synthesize fake options or trigger silent tool-schema errors when options=[] is rejected. No adapter.prompt_free_text primitive exists.
remediation_sketch: Introduce adapter.prompt_free_text sibling primitive. Split rule into bounded-choice (AskUserQuestion) vs free-text (prompt_free_text). Document command split. Ship before next release.

excerpt: Mode Profile Resolution ... 1. Read .planning/config/control-modes.yaml 2. Look up profiles[control_mode] ... 4. Pass resolved profile to authority-enforcer and wave-executor integration points
issue: Three Implicit Preconditions: (1) downstream consumers (authority-enforcer, wave-executor) must accept the 5-flag schema but no contract verification; (2) key lookup case sensitivity unspecified; (3) partial profile (subset of 5 flags present) handling unspecified. 4.7 may silently pass malformed profiles.
remediation_sketch: Specify: key lookup is case-sensitive; partial profiles warn+fallback to hardcoded guarded; 5-flag schema is contract. Add precondition assert before pass.

excerpt: Context Budget Ceiling (Core) ... build: soft 180 KB, hard 225 KB ... Release checks enforce hard ceilings and print telemetry for every command.
issue: HOW specified (KB numbers) but no WHY or action. Soft vs hard undefined — does soft trigger warning, pruning, or abort? Other 11 commands from L105-122 table have no budget entries — no rule for their treatment. 'Release checks enforce' defers to unspecified external process.
remediation_sketch: Front-load goal: prevent context overflow. Soft triggers telemetry warning; hard causes abort. Unlisted commands inherit soft 120/hard 150. Define enforcement location.

excerpt: Activate only when phase requirements or task context indicates domain relevance.
issue: Entire activation trigger is 'when phase requirements or task context indicates domain relevance'. 'Indicates domain relevance' has no enumeration, no canonical keyword list, no fallback. All 4 rules total do not define what 'domain relevance' means operationally. Parity with S02c cross-cut — marketing/design keyword registry absent from intent-teams.yaml. 4.7 either always activates (skill is loaded) or never activates (unprovable).
remediation_sketch: Concrete rule: Activate when (a) phase has requirement ID matching ^(MKT|DSN)-\d+, OR (b) user passed --domain=marketing|design, OR (c) phase frontmatter domain:=marketing|design. Drop 'indicates domain relevance' entirely.

excerpt: Require both: gh auth status success and a valid git remote.
issue: Precondition gate is underspecified relative to parent workflow-common Sec 649-655 which requires three checks: 'which gh' AND 'gh auth status' AND GitHub-hosted remote. Readers loading only this extension miss the 'which gh' check and hostname verification — 'git remote get-url origin' passes for any remote including GitLab/Bitbucket. Silent failures with confusing errors follow.
remediation_sketch: Expand rule 2 to three-part check or reference github-sync Section 1 as canonical verifier instead of duplicating here.

excerpt: Always reflect GitHub updates in local state files when relevant.
issue: 'when relevant' is textbook CAT-2 ambiguous trigger per rubric. No enumeration of relevance criteria. Which state files? Which events qualify? 4.7 either updates on every call (noisy, corrupting) or never updates (defeats rule).
remediation_sketch: Explicit: After any GitHub write op (create/update/close issue/PR, close milestone), mirror result to .planning/STATE.md ## GitHub within same invocation. Read-only ops do not trigger updates.

excerpt: Missing memory files must not block command execution.
issue: Graceful-degradation contract collapsed to one rule without the canonical 4-step check pattern from parent workflow-common L606-611. No enumeration of the 4 memory files (OUTCOMES/PATTERNS/ERRORS/PREFERENCES) — reader may assume single file. 4.7 loading this extension without the parent may attempt ops before existence check, producing errors this rule is meant to prevent.
remediation_sketch: Expand rule 3 to explicit 4-step: check .planning/memory/{file}.md exists where file in {OUTCOMES,PATTERNS,ERRORS,PREFERENCES}; if exists use, if not skip silently with log, never raise. Reference parent canonical section.

excerpt: Record outcomes/preferences only when workflow explicitly calls for it.
issue: Circular trigger — 'record when told to record'. Parent workflow-common L590-604 enumerates 14 concrete memory integration points; this extension does not reference them. 4.7 defaults to either never record (safe interpretation of 'only when') or record at every opportunity.
remediation_sketch: Replace with: 'Record memory only at the integration points listed in workflow-common § Memory Integration Points (14 canonical points across build/review/plan/status). Do not record outside these.'

excerpt: For MULTIPLE plans in the wave: Issue ALL agent spawn calls simultaneously per adapter.coordinate_parallel ... Do NOT spawn agents one at a time for multi-plan waves
issue: Core dispatch authority. 'Issue ALL agent spawn calls simultaneously' does not specify the mechanism — Claude Code parallel execution requires multiple tool calls in the SAME LLM response, not sequential calls across turns. 4.7's documented regression is defaulting to fewer subagents; without explicit 'single response block' instruction, 4.7 reads 'simultaneously' as 'as fast as possible, one at a time' which is the observed failure. Parent finding cited by LEGION-47-023 (commands/build.md).
remediation_sketch: Rewrite Step 4: 'For multi-plan waves, issue N Agent tool calls within a SINGLE LLM response block — do not split across turns. Your response contains exactly N Agent tool invocations in one tool_use array. If you issue call 1 then wait before issuing call 2, STOP — you serialized the wave.' Add counter-example and post-dispatch verification.

excerpt: wave: A | B # Wave A or Wave B ... wave_role: build | analysis | execution | remediation
issue: Section 7 Two-Wave Plan Structure uses wave: as a STRING (A/B) while Section 2 Step 3 L174 declares wave: as integer. Schema contradiction for same frontmatter field. Two-Wave Activation rules L1584 are underspecified ('plans span multiple service/page groups OR include explicit analysis tasks' — no enumeration). 4.7 cannot determine when to switch int→str semantics; parallel dispatch breaks either way.
remediation_sketch: Pick one schema: (a) unify on integer waves (A=1, B=2, drop string) or (b) explicit two_wave: true frontmatter flag switches interpretation. Document auto-activation detection or require explicit opt-in. Cross-reference plan-frontmatter.schema.json.

excerpt: Two-wave pattern activates when: ... Plans span multiple service/page groups OR include explicit analysis tasks ... Standard single-wave execution when: ... All plans touch same files
issue: Three unenumerated triggers gating major execution-mode switch: 'service/page groups' undefined; 'explicit analysis tasks' — no detection rule; 'All plans touch same files' — ambiguous (any, full, >50% overlap?). If two_wave: true override set but detection says single-wave, no priority rule.
remediation_sketch: Replace heuristic triggers: 'Two-wave activates ONLY when two_wave: true in phase CONTEXT.md. Auto-detection disabled.' OR specify: 'Auto-activate when plan count >= 4 AND any plan has wave_role: set. The two_wave override ALWAYS wins.' Drop service/page groups prose.

excerpt: Section 5.7: SUMMARY.md Export Validation ... For each missing required section: - Log advisory warning ... This is advisory, NOT blocking
issue: Validator has no done state. After logging warnings, unclear when Section 5.7 is complete. Validation report location (Section 4 Step 6 wave completion vs 5.7 Step 5) is inconsistent — 4 Step 6 doesn't list validation as required section. Graceful-degradation philosophy collides with Principle 12 'verification-gated completion' — which wins for SUMMARY.md structural validation vs task verification?
remediation_sketch: Specify done state: 'Section 5.7 complete when (1) every SUMMARY.md read+checked; (2) validation line printed per plan to wave completion; (3) warnings captured in ## Advisories sub-section. Does not block wave; separate from Principle 12 task verification which DOES block.'

excerpt: Step 1.5: ... Extract the suffix after the last hyphen-delimited division prefix (e.g., 'dev-senior-developer' -> 'senior-developer', 'backend-architect' -> 'backend-architect') ... d) If MULTIPLE matches: pick the first ... e) If ZERO matches ... fall back to autonomous mode
issue: Fuzzy-match cascade has three defects: (1) division prefix list unenumerated; inconsistent examples ('backend-architect' -> 'backend-architect' implies 'backend' is not a prefix); (2) 'pick the first' is filesystem-order-dependent non-deterministic; (3) autonomous-mode fallback here contradicts Section 2 Step 5(f) and Section 6 Error #5 which both say STOP with error. Three sections give three instructions for same condition. Matches LEGION-47-052 defect class.
remediation_sketch: (a) Unify fallback across Sections 2, 3, 6 — pick STOP per Section 6 rationale. (b) Enumerate prefix list or reference agent-registry CATALOG.md. (c) Replace 'pick the first' with 'STOP: ambiguous agent-id matched N candidates'. (d) Consider removing fuzzy match entirely — it is the defect, not the fix.

excerpt: When worktree mode is active: Principle 8 (Files isolation per wave) is relaxed — worktrees provide filesystem-level isolation instead of plan-level file list disjointness
issue: Worktree mode silently relaxes Principle 8 safety invariant via single boolean execution.use_worktrees in settings.json. No human-approval gate per CLAUDE.md authority matrix. 4.7 sees 'when worktree mode is active' as unconditional state check — does not verify user consent. Authority boundary ambiguous.
remediation_sketch: Add gate: 'Worktree mode requires (a) execution.use_worktrees: true AND STATE.md ## Consent section worktree_mode_consent: true, OR (b) explicit --worktrees flag on current invocation. If neither, treat as false regardless of settings.' Add initial-activation prompt via adapter.ask_user.

excerpt: ## Section 6: INTENT-BASED PLAN FILTERING ... ## Step 3.5: APPLY INTENT FILTERS (conditional) ... ## Section 10: File Placement Utilities (ENV-04)
issue: Structural defect: file has TWO Section 6 headings (Error Scenarios L1370, INTENT-BASED PLAN FILTERING L1629). Jumps from Section 7 (L1486) to Section 10 (L1722); no Sections 8 or 9. 'Step 3.5: APPLY INTENT FILTERS' (L1679) belongs to Section 4 but lives below Section 7. References table L1596 lists Section 6 = Error Handling, contradicting the later Section 6 header. 4.7 scanning 'Section 6' gets two contradictory results.
remediation_sketch: Renumber: Section 6 INTENT-BASED -> Section 8, Section 10 -> Section 9. Or move intent-filtering into Section 2 (Plan Discovery) and file-placement into Appendix A. Move Step 3.5 back into Section 4. Audit all cross-file references to Section 6/8/9/10 and update.

excerpt: ## Section 7: Two-Wave Pattern ... Enhanced execution model with distinct Build/Analysis and Execution waves for maximum parallelism.
issue: Goal stated ('maximum parallelism') but operational intent not front-loaded. Mechanic described before reader knows WHY this particular partition is correct. No decision procedure for classifying plans into build/analysis/execution/remediation. wave_role frontmatter has no rule for who sets it or how. Compounds LEGION-47-102 schema conflict.
remediation_sketch: Front-load intent: 'GOAL: Section 7 for phases needing explicit architecture review between build and validation. Decision: (1) if any two plans must produce artifacts that other plans review before execution, use Section 7; (2) assign wave_role: by role.' Integrate with Section 2 wave map.

excerpt: 9. AGENT COMPLETES BUT DOESN'T REPORT ... Detection: Agent appears idle but no completion report was received ... If the agent still doesn't respond: infer the result from the filesystem
issue: 'Agent appears idle' has no quantified threshold. 'Wait for the agent's response' — how long? Inference fallback silently upgrades no-response to 'Complete with Warnings' potentially fooling downstream. Filesystem evidence approach does not distinguish 'completed and forgot' from 'partially completed and hung'.
remediation_sketch: Add thresholds: 'idle = no adapter.collect_results within 60s after last activity. Send follow-up and wait 30s more. Infer from filesystem ONLY IF files_modified complete AND all <verification> pass; else Status: Failed with inconclusive.'

excerpt: Part 1: Agent personality - Read the ENTIRE agent .md file: agents/{agent-id}.md - Capture as PERSONALITY_CONTENT ... Assemble the final prompt using this exact template: {PERSONALITY_CONTENT} ... {TASK_DESCRIPTION} {RESULT_INSTRUCTION}
issue: Re-filing S05 deferred cross-cut. model_tier declared in agent-registry:147, passed by phase-decomposer:459, set to execution by review-evaluators:1259 — but cli-dispatch Section 3 never reads it. Prompt template has 5 parts (PERSONALITY, TASK, RESULT, CONTROL, HANDOFF) with no model_tier lookup. Claude-Code adapter hardcodes model: {model_execution}. Net: all tiers collapse to sonnet. Maintainers adding model_tier accumulate dead metadata.
remediation_sketch: Path A: add Part 6 reading model_tier from agent frontmatter (default execution), map to adapter.model_{tier}, substitute into spawn row. Update adapters' spawn_agent_personality rows to use {model} not hardcoded {model_execution}. Path B: delete model_tier declarations. Path A is correct long-term.

excerpt: Read the ENTIRE agent .md file: agents/{agent-id}.md

excerpt: For parallel dispatches (multiple tasks in the same wave): Use run_in_background: true on the Bash tool call. Do NOT use run_in_background for single-task dispatches

Same CAT-3 family as LEGION-47-101. Also 'Do NOT use run_in_background for single-task' contradicts workflow-common output-redirection convention L481-503 which has no task-count restriction.
remediation_sketch: Specify: 'For N parallel dispatches, issue N Bash tool calls in SAME LLM response block, each with run_in_background: true. Same-response Bash calls run concurrently. Next response issue single wait call OR poll result files with bounded timeout. Do not split N background spawns across turns — this serializes.'
